name: Dllhost.exe Execution Anomaly
notes:
This rule detects the dllhost.exe process spawning with no command line arguments which is rare and could indicate process injection activity or malware mimicking similar system processes.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
SIGMA Title: Dllhost.EXE Execution Anomaly
SIGMA Description: Detects a "dllhost" process spawning with no commandline arguments which is very rare to happen and could indicate process injection activity or malware mimicking similar system processes.


Confidence: MEDIUM

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches Command (custom) is not N/A
when the event matches LOWER("Process Full Full Name") MATCHES 'dllhost\.exe' AQL filter query
when the event matches LOWER("Command") == 'dllhost' OR LOWER("Command") == 'dllhost.exe' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Dllhost.exe Execution Anomaly on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches Command (custom) is not N/A
AND when the event matches LOWER("Process Full Full Name") MATCHES 'dllhost\.exe' AQL filter query
AND when the event matches LOWER("Command") == 'dllhost' OR LOWER("Command") == 'dllhost.exe' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event description: Detects the dllhost.exe process spawning with no command line arguments which is rare and could indicate process injection activity or malware mimicking similar system processes.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
